classObjective: Classify angle pairs formed by two lines cut by a transversal as alternate interior, alternate exterior, corresponding, or consecutive interior angles.
workedExamples: 12-7, Example 1 — Identify Parallel, Skew, and Perpendicular Segments
12-7, Example 2 — Identify Transversals and Classify Angle Pairs
12-7, Example 3 — Classify Angle Pairs Formed by a Transversal
warmUp: What are the four main angle pair types when a transversal crosses parallel lines?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: angle pair vocabulary; identifying transversals; alternate interior/exterior, corresponding, consecutive interior angles
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice classifying angle pairs formed by transversals
independentPractice: Practice problems identifying and classifying all angle pair types in transversal diagrams
assessment: Exit ticket: identify all angle pairs (corresponding, alternate interior, alternate exterior, consecutive interior) in a given transversal diagram
capReflection: How does the location of angles relative to the transversal help you remember their classifications?
